description: Focused routing fixture for change-comprehension. Positive cases assert both modes route to the skill. Negative controls assert that neighboring planning, review, verification, debugging, and generic-explanation intents keep their existing owners and do NOT activate change comprehension — the boundary that matters, since the skill must never volunteer itself.
notes: The runner scores lexical token overlap against skill name + description, per skill, against a fixed threshold. It has no notion of ranking, so `avoid` on a positive case asserts a sibling scores below threshold in absolute terms. Several siblings carry generic vocabulary in their own descriptions (write-spec's description contains both 'change' and 'what'; local-review's contains 'diff'), so any realistic scope or quiz prompt lands them above threshold no matter how change-comprehension is worded. Asserting otherwise would be testing sibling descriptions, not this skill. Positive cases therefore assert only that change-comprehension triggers; over-triggering is asserted by the eight sibling negative controls plus the generic-explanation control below.

cases.prompt: Before we implement this, help me understand the scope of the proposed change and its blast radius.
Walk me through what this change touches so I have a mental model of it before you build.
Quiz me on this change before I merge it so I know I actually understand it.
Test my comprehension of the diff you just implemented and correct me where I'm wrong.
Use $change-comprehension to brief me on what this refactor affects.
I'm not sure what we should build here. Let's talk through the options and trade-offs one at a time.
Pin the falsifiable contract and acceptance criteria for this feature before anyone writes code.
Sequence the implementation into ordered tasks with the files to touch and verification commands.
Which architecture should we pick here? Reason from first principles about the trade-offs.
This endpoint throws intermittently in production. Debug it and find the root cause.
Review my staged diff and give me severity-ranked findings with file and line references.
Review pull request 42 on GitHub, check CI, and post a merge recommendation.
Before you claim this is done, show me the evidence that it actually passes.
